learning_sequence_context.position_in_sequence: Lesson 5 of 10 in the Term 1-2 sequence - Lesson 3 of the 8-lesson PowerPoint unit.
learning_sequence_context.previous_lesson_summary: Last lesson pupils transferred their 'About Me' mind map into PowerPoint using outline view, producing slide titles and bullet points.
learning_sequence_context.next_lesson_link: Next lesson pupils apply a theme, transitions and animations to the slides they have just illustrated.

expected_outcomes.lesson_objective: Pupils know how to find copyright-free images online, understand the range of image manipulation techniques available in PowerPoint, and can manipulate and insert images into their 'About Me' presentation.
assessment_criteria: Each student will find and insert at least one copyright-free image per slide from an appropriate source.
Each student will apply at least one manipulation technique (crop, resize, or style effect) to an inserted image.
Each student will be able to explain why a chosen image must be copyright-free.
expected_outcomes.activity_summary: Individual sourcing of copyright-free images and application of image manipulation techniques to illustrate each slide of pupils' 'About Me' PowerPoint.
expected_outcomes.cross_curricular_or_real_world_link: Understanding copyright applies to any use of online images or media beyond this lesson, including work in other subjects and personal use online.

key_vocabulary.definition: An image or media file that is free to use and reuse without infringing the creator's rights, e.g. released under a suitable licence.
To cut away unwanted parts of an image; everyday meaning relates to farming.

preparation.additional_adult_role: Support pupils who struggle to find a relevant copyright-free image, offering a pre-vetted alternative source list.
accessibility: Pre-vetted image source list available for pupils who need extra support searching independently

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Ask pupils to open their PowerPoint and count how many slides currently have no image.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Open their PowerPoint and note the number of slides needing an image.

check_for_understanding.question: Why do we use PowerPoint rather than just talking through our ideas?
check_for_understanding.decision_rule: Proceed if most pupils give a reasonable answer (visual support, structure); otherwise briefly recap.

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Explain what copyright-free means and why it matters, then demonstrate searching for and downloading a copyright-free image from an approved source.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Watch the demonstration and identify what makes a source trustworthy for copyright-free images.

check_for_understanding.question: Why can't we just use any image we find in a search engine?
check_for_understanding.decision_rule: Move to modelling manipulation regardless; readdress copyright if the class is confused.

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Model inserting an image into a slide, then cropping, resizing, and applying a style effect, thinking aloud about how each change affects the slide's appearance.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Watch and note one manipulation technique they want to try first.

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Set the task of sourcing and inserting a copyright-free image on each slide, then manipulating at least one; circulate with least-help-first prompts.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Find, insert, and manipulate copyright-free images across their 'About Me' slides.

check_for_understanding.question: Have you applied at least one manipulation technique to one of your images?
check_for_understanding.decision_rule: Pupils with no manipulation applied by the 15-minute mark are shown the technique again individually.

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Facilitate a paired swap-and-check for appropriate, well-manipulated images; cold-call two pairs to share one image choice and why it suits the slide.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Swap with a partner, check images are relevant and manipulated appropriately, and give one piece of feedback.

check_for_understanding.question: Exit ticket: name one manipulation technique you used and which slide you used it on.
check_for_understanding.decision_rule: Pupils unable to name a technique are flagged for a brief recap at the start of next lesson.
